vehicle.label: 6-DOF Industrial Arm
vehicle.desc: 6-DOF serial industrial robot arm, 5kg payload, 600mm reach

parts.label: Base Joint (J1)
parts.spec: Base rotary joint J1, +-180deg, harmonic drive, 100Nm peak torque, mounting flange ISO 9409

parts.label: Shoulder Joint (J2)
parts.spec: Shoulder pitch joint J2, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 80Nm, cable routing through center bore

parts.label: Upper Arm (Link 2-3)
parts.spec: Upper arm link, 300mm length, hollow tube with cable conduit, lightweight Al 7075

parts.label: Elbow Joint (J3)
parts.spec: Elbow pitch joint J3, +-150deg, cycloidal reducer, 50Nm, encoder dual-stack

parts.label: Forearm + Wrist (J4)
parts.spec: Forearm with wrist roll J4, 250mm length, +-360deg continuous, slip ring for power/signal

parts.label: Wrist Pitch (J5)
parts.spec: Wrist pitch joint J5, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 20Nm, compact form factor

parts.label: Wrist Roll (J6)
parts.spec: Wrist final roll joint J6, +-360deg, harmonic drive, 10Nm, ISO 9409-1 50mm tool flange

parts.label: Tool Flange + Changer
parts.spec: Tool changer master plate at J6 end, pneumatic locking, 4 utility ports

parts.label: Cable Management Spine
parts.spec: External cable carrier from base to wrist, drag chain or spiral, signal + power + pneumatic

parts.label: Replaceable Motor Module Set
parts.spec: Actuation_motors closure module: replaceable motor_module cartridges for J1-J6, fastener_access, coupling_dummy, insert/washer preload pads, torque_Nm intent labels

parts.label: Encoder Cover + Sensing Set
parts.spec: Sensing_encoders closure module: encoder_pocket and encoder_cover at each joint, index marks, cable exit strain relief, inspection labels

parts.label: Drive Electronics Bay
parts.spec: Drive_electronics bay with driver_bay, power_channel, signal_channel, ground_point, service cover, connector keying, thermal inspection window

requirements: Design a forearm with a wrist roll joint (J4) for a 6-DOF industrial robot arm.
The forearm should be 250mm long and capable of continuous rotation (+-360deg).
Include a slip ring for power and signal transmission.

verify.rule: All required fields are present.
The estimated mass does not exceed the allocated budget.
Each physical feature is traceable to its corresponding ID in the part_tree.
Interfaces with adjacent modules are reciprocal and clearly defined.

risk.desc: Potential thermal issues due to heat dissipation from motor and harmonic drive.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management solutions such as cooling fins or heat sinks.

risk.desc: Risk of improper assembly due to complex fastener patterns.
risk.mit: Provide detailed assembly instructions and training for technicians.

risk.desc: Cable routing may cause strain on harnesses if not properly managed.
risk.mit: Ensure proper strain relief and routing channels for cables.

risk.desc: Insufficient documentation for critical joints and fastener patterns.
risk.mit: Enhance documentation to include detailed descriptions of locking methods and preload intent.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to complex interfaces.
risk.mit: Use precise manufacturing techniques and quality control measures.

generated_parts.label: Cable Management Spine
generated_parts.spec: External cable carrier from base to wrist, drag chain or spiral, signal + power + pneumatic

requirements: External cable carrier from base to wrist, drag chain or spiral, signal + power + pneumatic
Cable spine with slack loops at every joint; connection types and load-path closure
Replaceable modules remain removable without full teardown
Harnesses routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling
J1-J6 axis rings, reducers/bearings, encoder covers, and tool flange datum are visible
Cable spine clips externally or remains inspectable through open channels
Joint cartridges can be removed without cutting printed links
Each joint shows a bearing seat with shoulder or retaining feature, shaft locating geometry, and stated fit intent
Harness crossing each joint has slack loop, strain relief at both ends, and routing that avoids pinch points
Every inter-part interface names a specific connection type with its DOF and a numeric clearance
Parts can be installed in a stated order where every fastener, connector, and mating feature stays reachable
One primary datum part anchors the assembly; each other part locates off a named parent datum using a 3-2-1 scheme
Every part's primary load traces through named interfaces to the base/ground; the load path closes for all parts
The design moves the right direction: fewer interfaces, parts consolidated where service is preserved
The assembly uses the fewest interfaces that still allow service and manufacture
Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent

verify.rule: All required fields are present.
Mass estimate is within the 150g limit.
Each required feature appears in the geometry operations.
Interfaces are reciprocal and clearly defined.
Critical joints have visible locking or preload evidence.

risk.mit: Implement larger slack loops and ensure proper bend radius.

risk.desc: Limited accessibility for some connectors.
risk.mit: Ensure all connectors are easily accessible without disassembly.

risk.desc: Risk of cable damage during installation.
risk.mit: Provide clear installation instructions and protective measures.
